Result: every docker/buildx INVOCATION in the capture/mirror scope is claimed by a pace — no orphans. PACE X gate ('a bind-mode build runs green'): the bind-mode build IS the rbtdro_onboarding_ordain_bind_plantuml case (onboarding.rs:1114), whose doc comment explicitly says it 'mirrors the digest into GAR via Cloud Build (skopeo from reliquary...)' — exactly the made-side bind mirror X converts skopeo->crane. BUT this case is in onboarding-sequence, which runs only in gauntlet/skirmish (cloud-spending release ladders), NOT in any service-tier suite. X's bind verify therefore requires a gauntlet or skirmish run, far more expensive than a service fixture; flag this as a verify-cost mismatch if X's docket implies a cheap gate.

PACE S false-green mechanism CONFIRMED. The chain: (1) BUK bud_dispatch.sh:122 sets BURD_PREVIOUS_DIR="${BURC_OUTPUT_ROOT_DIR}/previous" and at dispatch start PROMOTES the prior current/ -> previous/ under one shared root (:132-143). (2) zrbfd_elect_base_anchor (rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:894) gates on `test -f "${BURD_PREVIOUS_DIR}/${RBF_FACT_LODE_TOUCHMARK}"`; on absence it logs 'No bole touchmark in the chain' and returns 0 — a silent no-op (:900-903). (3) theurge rbtdri_invoke_impl (rbtdri_invocation.rs:254) gives EACH invoke its own isolated burv_output: burv_output = ctx.burv_output_root.join(rbtdri_invoke_dir_name(invoke_num)) with invoke_count incremented per call (:260-264), and passes that distinct dir as BURV_OUTPUT_ROOT_DIR (:275). Because each tabtarget invoke gets a FRESH distinct root, the previous/ promotion under invoke N+1's root never sees invoke N's touchmark — the ensconce's touchmark fact lands in invoke N's root, ordain runs in invoke N+1's separate root whose previous/ is empty. So election ALWAYS no-ops in theurge, the airgap-chain case never exercises bole derived-pull election, and it passes regardless. S's fix touches zrbfd_elect_base_anchor (rbfd) + rbtdri_invoke_impl burv_output isolation (theurge crate) — exactly the two sites S's docket names.

SPEC STATUS for W: RBSPV-PodmanVmSupplyChain.adoc is in Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/FUTURE/ (confirms W's 'promote RBSPV out of FUTURE/'). RBSL subdocs RBSLA/B/C/D/E/U exist (augur/banish/conclave/divine/ensconce/underpin) but there is NO podvm RBSL subdoc yet (W's 'RBSL podvm subdoc' is greenfield).
result.summary: Theurge has 28 registered fixtures (RBTDRC_FIXTURES, rbtdrc_crucible.rs:2506). Of the Lode-capture verify gates: lode-lifecycle, reliquary-lifecycle, and wsl-lifecycle ALL EXIST and are ALL service-tier (and complete-tier) — so paces T, U, and V have express, already-built service-tier verify gates with no hidden test-authoring scope. The only MISSING fixture is podvm-lifecycle (pace W's gate), which is confirmed absent everywhere and is explicitly owned by W's greenfield docket — so it is not an orphan and not hidden scope. Two findings warrant flagging: (1) Pace X's skopeo-cohort removal will break the reliquary-lifecycle fixture, which currently asserts the rbi_skopeo cohort member tag (rbtdrc_crucible.rs:3360, const :2957) — X must update that fixture, which is hidden test-maintenance scope not in X's quoted site list. (2) Pace X's 'bind-mode build runs green' gate is the bind-plantuml case inside onboarding-sequence, which runs only in the gauntlet/skirmish cloud-spending ladders, not any service-tier suite — a verify-cost mismatch if X expects a cheap gate. Pace S's 'airgap-chain fixture' is actually a CASE inside the onboarding-sequence fixture (gauntlet/skirmish only), and the false-green mechanism is fully confirmed: theurge's rbtdri_invoke_impl isolates each tabtarget invoke in its own burv_output root, so BUK's current/->previous/ promotion never carries the bole touchmark across invokes, making zrbfd_elect_base_anchor silently no-op and the airgap-chain case pass without ever exercising bole derived-pull election. S's two named sites (zrbfd_elect_base_anchor in rbfd, rbtdri_invoke_impl burv_output isolation in theurge) are exactly the fix points.

capability_findings: gcrane IS the correct binary, not plain crane. Plain crane authenticates ONLY via authn.DefaultKeychain (docker config file) which is empty on a Cloud Build worker — it would FAIL against *.pkg.dev without an explicit 'crane auth login' + metadata token. gcrane uses google.Keychain (ADC -> GCE metadata server = Mason SA) and auths GAR ambiently. Memo §9 (memo-20260608-lode-podvm-cerebro-experiment.md:286-301) states this binary distinction explicitly and calls it 'load-bearing'.

CONFIRMED PRESENT in gcrane (memo §9:292 + RBSCB:70 + rbld0_Lode.sh:74 all say 'same cp/manifest/tag engine'; memo §9 adds 'digest'): cp, manifest, tag, digest. gcrane is a superset of crane (same go-containerregistry repo), so any crane subcommand exists in gcrane.

APPEND IS NOT VERIFIED ANYWHERE IN THE REPO. Searched memo, all specs, all snippets: zero occurrences of 'crane append' or 'gcrane append'. Pace V's plan ('buildx -> crane append for two FROM-scratch builds') relies on a subcommand that the empirical cerebro experiment (memo §4 tool-verdict table) tested only for cp/manifest, NOT append. crane append DOES exist in real-world go-containerregistry (appends tarball layers to a base, supports --base scratch via empty base), and gcrane being a superset would carry it — BUT this is asserted from general knowledge, not from any repo evidence. RECOMMEND: pace V should empirically confirm 'gcrane append' on gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug before committing, especially that it can produce a FROM-scratch single-layer image (append with no base / --base= empty) and push it. This is the single highest capability risk in the plan.

gcrane cp = the 'skopeo copy --all' replacement for pace X: memo §4 table + rbgjl01 comment confirm 'gcrane cp copies the whole reference (manifest list and all per-platform manifests)' digest-byte-identical. No separate --all flag needed; crane cp copies the full index by default. Multi-platform preservation CONFIRMED empirically (memo §4: qemu 1.13GB leaf, byte-identical digest).

gcrane cp also cleanly replaces conclave's 'docker pull + docker tag + docker push' (pace U): a single registry-to-registry cp, no local daemon, no buildx. The skopeo|quay.io/skopeo entry in the conclave MANIFEST is a cohort tool-REF in data (allowed by pace D); it is the conclave's captured-cohort membership, not an invocation.

TERMINOLOGY RISK — REAL but MITIGATED by one strong anchor. The downstream dockets (U/V/W/X) and the FUTURE RBSPV spec use loose 'crane' / 'crane cp' / 'crane append' wording. A mount agent reading ONLY the docket could reach for plain crane (no GAR auth -> silent/late failure against *.pkg.dev). WHY gcrane NOT crane IS LOAD-BEARING (verified via RBSCB:70-71): plain crane authenticates only via authn.DefaultKeychain (docker config), which is EMPTY on a Cloud Build worker — it would FAIL against *.pkg.dev. gcrane uses google.Keychain -> ADC -> GCE metadata server (Mason SA), authing GAR ambiently with no token fetch, no 'crane auth login', no credential-helper image. A mount agent who reaches for plain crane gets a silent/late auth failure.
